mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and overall economic health, which typically translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive, but SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, but SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low federal funds rates and increasing housing starts indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting a recovering or expanding housing market, which is a key driver of broader economic growth and equity market performance over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0% and HOUST shows sustained growth above 5% for 3 months, but SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production combined with robust GDP growth during an economic expansion indicates strong manufacturing activity and corporate profitability, which typically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth > 2%
failure_condition: If industrial production growth exceeds 3% annualized for 3 months during an expansionary GDP period, but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of these instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in real GDP over two consecutive quarters signals a recessionary environment, leading to widespread declines in corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns due to increased risk aversion.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows two consecutive quarters of negative annualized growth, but SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of the subsequent periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent and positive growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, but SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high and accelerating consumer price inflation alongside rising federal funds rates indicates aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates, reduces corporate profitability, and typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 4% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25%
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annualized 3-month change exceeds 4% and FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25% over 3 months, but SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in housing starts following a period of decline signals renewed confidence in the economy and a recovery in the housing sector, which often precedes broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns, especially in a post-recession environment.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average growth > 5% after a 6-month period of decline
failure_condition: If HOUST shows a 3-month average growth above 5% after a prior decline, but SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of these recovery instances.
